jobs.title: Tax Assistant Intern
jobs.descriptionHtml:
About Ledger

We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.

Reporting to our Tax Specialist, you will actively participate in the Group's daily tax management. Your main responsibilities will include:

What you’ll be doing:

1. Indirect Tax Support (VAT & Customs)

  • Compliance: Assist in the preparation and review of VAT returns across the various countries where we operate.

  • Trade Reporting: Participate in filing Intrastat declarations for goods (DEB) and services (DES).

2. Innovation Tax (R&D Tax Credit)

  • Documentation: Support the collection of data from technical teams for the Research Tax Credit (CIR) justification file.

  • Tracking: Help monitor eligible time logs and R&D expenditures.

3. Transfer Pricing & Compliance

  • Documentation: Assist in updating the group’s transfer pricing documentation (Master File / Local Files).

  • Tax Watch: Conduct international tax research to anticipate legislative changes in our key markets.

4. Cross-functional Projects

  • Process Improvement: Participate in optimizing internal processes and automating tax reporting within our ERP.

  • Reform Implementation: Assist in the rollout of the French electronic invoicing reform (e-invoicing)

What we’re looking for:

  • Education: Master’s student (M1 or M2) in Tax Law, Business School with a tax specialization or preparing for the Bar Exam (CAPA).

  • Solid understanding of VAT mechanisms (ideally regarding EU intra-community flows and exports).

  • High proficiency in Excel (VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables).

  • Languages: A good level of English is required for daily communication with international partners.

  • Soft Skills: Rigor, intellectual curiosity, analytical mindset, and a strong ability to work as part of a team.

At Ledger, we are dedicated to continually investing in our employees which is why we offer more than just salaries; we provide comprehensive compensation packages that include a wide range of benefits.

For regionally specific benefits, your Talent Acquisition contact will be able to provide you with more information.

We’re committed to building an inclusive hiring process. If you need any adjustments or accommodations, just let us know, we’ll do our best to support you.

jobs.title: Senior Software Architect, Blockchain & DeFi
jobs.descriptionHtml:
About Ledger

We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.

The team:

As part of a strong growth we are looking for talent to join the Engineering Architecture team. The architecture team is responsible for ensuring the highest level of design quality for both our B2C and B2B products, as well as helping the Product teams scope and build the next great features of our products.

The architecture team aims at building deep, specialized expertise in Ledger's transactional services, and in this context we are looking at reinforcing our team with a future expert to drive the architecture, integration, and improvement of our core services, with a strong focus on new Blockchain protocols and Decentralized Finance (DeFi) applications.

This is a hands-on position and we expect our architects to be able to dig deep into the code and help engineering teams in their day to day issues.

The team you will be joining has the following missions:

  • Support Product & Engineering on Discovery & Design of new product features

  • Drive Tech Initiatives with Engineering to improve our stack, address tech debt and support good engineering practices

  • Support engineering teams on critical topics by providing expertise and hands-on contribution to engineering squads

  • Explore new, innovative domains with a focus on concrete Ledger applications, particularly in the Web3/DeFi space

What you’ll be doing:

The responsibilities of an architect are mostly geared toward the engineering teams they support:

  • Lead the technical scoping, design, and architecture for integrating new blockchain protocols and tokens into the Ledger ecosystem.

  • Drive the research, understanding, and integration of complex Decentralized Finance (DeFi) protocols (e.g., DEXs, lending, staking) with Ledger services, ensuring security and scalability.

  • Collaborate closely with engineering leads, product managers and delivery managers to ensure design on most important initiatives are properly done and of good quality.

  • Partner with product teams to define, develop and implement development requirements for new product features.

  • Help Engineering managers identify, tackle and prioritize most critical technical debt in our transactional services stack.

  • Contribute hands on to the development activities of the squads being supported.

  • Become a Ledger solution expert to thoroughly understand all use-case scenarios, especially on areas identified in this job description.

An architect is also responsible for some transversal activities, including:

  • Be involved in our open source projects and foster our developer community by contributing and maintaining key Open Source software, and helping build our platform strategy.

  • Help build visibility on the roadmap by contributing to gross estimates of implementation difficulty.

  • Help contribute to Ledger visibility by presenting in conferences or in front of customers our technology know-how.

What we’re looking for:

Requirements

  • 10+ years of experience in technical design, development, execution, testing and delivery.

  • Deep expertise/interest in backend system architecture, algorithm development, and optimization for high-throughput, high-reliability transactional services.

  • Profound, hands-on knowledge of how various blockchain technologies (e.g., Ethereum, EVMs, non-EVMs) work under the hood, and a curiosity to investigate deeply complex, distributed systems.

  • Ability to work effectively with internal teams to define scope, goals, project plans and deliverables.

  • Team player with excellent interpersonal skills and ability to work cross-functionally.

  • Excellent presentation and written communication.

  • Engineering degree.

Requirements based on target engineering team

  • Expert knowledge of backend development and architecting scalable, secure web services (e.g., Node.js, Python, Scala).

  • Experience with advanced software development practices and patterns for distributed, fault-tolerant systems.

  • Deep understanding of smart contract security, token standards (ERC-20, ERC-721, etc.), and the architecture of common DeFi primitives (DEXs, Lending Protocols, Yield Farming).

  • Practical experience with blockchain node interaction (RPC, WebSockets) and building robust, high-availability data indexing and transaction services.

  • Strong knowledge of database technologies (SQL/NoSQL) and message queues for asynchronous data processing in backend systems.

Nice to have

  • Open source experience or contributions, especially in the blockchain/crypto space.

  • Expert knowledge of cryptography and security practices applied to transactional systems.

  • Prior experience contributing to or integrating with major DeFi protocols.

At Ledger, we are dedicated to continually investing in our employees which is why we offer more than just salaries; we provide comprehensive compensation packages that include a wide range of benefits. Here are some of the benefits you can look forward to:

  • Flexible work options - Our hybrid policy allows employees to work from home up to 3 times per week

  • Health & Wellness support - Health and Life Insurance.

  • Financial growth opportunities - Employees can become shareholders in Ledger as well as other financial benefits depending on your country of work.

  • Commuter allowance - Ledger offers a commuter allowance to contribute to your preferred means of transportation.

  • Learning & Development - A comprehensive suite of training solutions providing a personalised learning experience for every employee.

For regionally specific benefits, your Talent Acquisition contact will be able to provide you with more information.

We’re committed to building an inclusive hiring process. If you need any adjustments or accommodations, just let us know, we’ll do our best to support you.

jobs.title: Senior Delivery Manager
jobs.descriptionHtml:
About Ledger

We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.


The team

We are looking for a Senior Delivery Manager to help us scale our engineering team. Our team is central to the engineering organization, perceived as a force multiplier by all the other engineers. We are constantly on the lookout to improve how we work. By joining our passionate, close-knitted team, you can have an impact in many different ways and learn in a team that is both super skilled and fast-growing!

Here is what the day-to-day of a Senior Delivery Manager in Ledger:

What you’ll be doing

  • Understand technical and product challenges, and represent the team towards 3rd party stakeholders.

  • Manage a clear schedule and risks for the team

  • Assist in the proactive management of dependencies and alignments across teams and domains (cross-domain) to ensure coherent program delivery

  • Identify, anticipate, challenge and help resolve impediments for the team

  • Coach the team in their continuous improvement and agile learning

  • Report efficiently and regularly on the delivery plans and dependency impacts

  • Support Product and Tech in their respective duties - Backlog management, grooming, planification, prioritization

  • Support the other Delivery Managers in monitoring metrics that give insight into the product, and drive positive change

  • Synthesize team progress status to provide clear visibility to stakeholders and middle management

What we’re looking for:

  • Strong expertise for software lifecycle management

  • 4+ years of practical program and project management skills, in traditional and agile practices

  • Proven track record in supporting the delivery of fast growing teams

  • Excellent communication skills written and verbal

  • JIRA is your friend

  • Familiarity or experience with Agile scaling frameworks (e.g., SAFe, LeSS)

  • Demonstrated experience in defining or improving key delivery performance indicators (KPIs) for development teams

  • Having a strong interest in cryptocurrencies and wishing to work with blockchain technology is an advantage

At Ledger, we are dedicated to continually investing in our employees which is why we offer more than just salaries; we provide comprehensive compensation packages that include a wide range of benefits. Here are some of the benefits you can look forward to:

  • Flexible work options - Our hybrid policy allows employees to work from home up to 3 times per week

  • Health & Wellness support - Health and Life Insurance.

  • Financial growth opportunities - Employees can become shareholders in Ledger as well as other financial benefits depending on your country of work.

  • Commuter allowance - Ledger offers a commuter allowance to contribute to your preferred means of transportation.

  • Learning & Development - A comprehensive suite of training solutions providing a personalised learning experience for every employee.

For regionally specific benefits, your Talent Acquisition contact will be able to provide you with more information.

jobs.title: Managed Services Operations Manager
jobs.descriptionHtml:
We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.

The team:

The Managed Services organization ensures the reliability, stability, and operational excellence of Ledger’s critical production platforms.

The MS Operations team is responsible for day-to-day service health, incident response, on-call readiness, and operational discipline across Ledger customer-facing environments. MS Operations partners closely with Platform Engineering and SRE teams to operate platforms and services, escalating complex or engineering-level issues to those teams when required.

As Managed Services Operations Manager, you will own operational outcomes, provide strong technical leadership, and manage the MS Operations Team(contractor-based) while working closely with Engineering and Infrastructure teams. This role reports to the Managed Services Director.

What you’ll be doing:

  • Own and lead the MS Operations function, including people management, priorities, and operational accountability.

  • Provide technical leadership on operational architecture, tooling, automation, and incident response.

  • Own day-to-day operational stability, operational readiness, and change validation for Ledger services.

  • Define and own the on-call operating model; act as operational lead during major incidents.

  • Act as the SME for operational observability, defining requirements and validating monitoring and alerting effectiveness.

  • Drive continuous improvement, reducing operational debt and improving reliability and efficiency.

  • Own and track key operational KPIs (MTTR, incident recurrence, on-call health).

  • Represent Managed Services Operations in cross-team operational and governance discussions.

  • Identify, assess, and escalate operational risks (capacity, tooling, skills, process gaps), with clear mitigation proposals to the Managed Services Director.

What we’re looking for:

  • Senior experience in production tech operations, SRE, or platform operations.

  • Proven leadership of tech operations teams with accountability for service stability.

  • Strong experience with cloud-native environments (AWS, Kubernetes, observability tooling).

  • Hands-on incident management and tech incident leadership experience.

  • Strong communication, ownership mindset, and ability to work cross-functionally.

Nice to have

  • Experience with contractor-based teams.

  • Exposure to high-availability, security-sensitive 24/7 production environments.

  • Experience working in multicultural and international team environments.

At Ledger, we are dedicated to continually investing in our employees which is why we offer more than just salaries; we provide comprehensive compensation packages that include a wide range of benefits. Here are some of the benefits you can look forward to:

  • Flexible work options - Our hybrid policy allows employees to work from home up to 3 times per week

  • Health & Wellness support - Health and Life Insurance.

  • Financial growth opportunities - Employees can become shareholders in Ledger as well as other financial benefits depending on your country of work.

  • Commuter allowance - Ledger offers a commuter allowance to contribute to your preferred means of transportation.

  • Learning & Development - A comprehensive suite of training solutions providing a personalised learning experience for every employee.

For regionally specific benefits, your Talent Acquisition contact will be able to provide you with more information.

We’re committed to building an inclusive hiring process. If you need any adjustments or accommodations, just let us know, we’ll do our best to support you.

jobs.title: Platform Engineering Director
jobs.descriptionHtml:
About Ledger

We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.

We are seeking an experienced Platform Engineering Director to manage and lead our Platform Engineering team within the Infrastructure & Operations department. This role reports directly to the VP Infrastructure & Operations and is critical in building and maintaining our cloud infrastructure as well as developer platforms and tooling. The ideal candidate will have a strong technical background, exceptional leadership skills, and a passion for building robust, scalable platform services while ensuring production infrastructure reliability.

What you’ll be doing:

Strategic Leadership

  • Define and execute the platform engineering strategy aligned with business objectives and Infrastructure & Operations goals

  • Lead and manage the Platform Engineering team to high performance

  • Establish and govern best practices, standards, and architecture for platform services and production cloud infrastructure

  • Partner with engineering leadership and Infrastructure & Operations teams to define platform and production system requirements

  • Act as the final escalation point for complex platform engineering issues beyond Level 2 operations support

Platform Development & Infrastructure

  • Oversee the design, development, and maintenance of developer platforms enabling application delivery, CI/CD, and deployment automation

  • Build and maintain platform infrastructure, cloud environments, and tooling in line with architectural standards and requirements

  • Design and operate scalable, highly available, and reliable production systems and services

  • Lead initiatives for infrastructure as code (IaC), observability, and monitoring across development and production environments

  • Provide expert-level technical leadership during complex platform, infrastructure, and production incidents

Team Management

  • Recruit, mentor, and develop platform engineers at all levels

  • Foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and continuous improvement

  • Manage team performance, career development, and succession planning

  • Allocate resources effectively across multiple platform initiatives

  • Build strong cross-functional relationships with Infrastructure & Operations, Engineering, Product, Security, and SRE teams

  • Provide architectural guidance and mentorship to SRE and Level 2 operations teams, including transition-to-support and advanced troubleshooting

Technical Excellence

  • Drive adoption of modern platform engineering practices (e.g. GitOps, service mesh, API gateways)

  • Ensure platform and production infrastructure security, compliance, and cost optimisation

  • Evaluate and integrate new technologies to enhance platform capabilities and production system reliability

  • Maintain technical documentation, architecture diagrams, and advanced operational runbooks

  • Lead post-mortems and root cause analysis for escalated platform and cloud infrastructure incidents, driving improvements with SRE teams

  • Design and own disaster recovery, backup, and high-availability strategies for cloud infrastructure

  • Collaborate with Service Quality Management (SQM) teams on incident and problem management

What we’re looking for:

  • 10+ years’ experience in software and platform/infrastructure engineering, including senior technical leadership

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent experience

  • Proven experience leading platform or infrastructure engineering teams, including managers and senior ICs, in distributed and matrixed environments

  • Deep expertise in AWS, Kubernetes, containerisation, infrastructure as code, and CI/CD/deployment automation

  • Experience designing, building, and operating developer platforms or internal PaaS

  • Knowledge of platform engineering patterns (e.g. golden paths, paved roads), service mesh, and API management

  • Strong background in production system architecture, capacity planning, performance optimisation, and incident leadership

  • Excellent communication skills with senior and executive stakeholders

At Ledger, we are dedicated to continually investing in our employees which is why we offer more than just salaries; we provide comprehensive compensation packages that include a wide range of benefits. Here are some of the benefits you can look forward to:

  • Flexible work options - Our hybrid policy allows employees to work from home up to 3 times per week

  • Health & Wellness support - Health and Life Insurance.

  • Financial growth opportunities - Employees can become shareholders in Ledger as well as other financial benefits depending on your country of work.

  • Commuter allowance - Ledger offers a commuter allowance to contribute to your preferred means of transportation.

  • Learning & Development - A comprehensive suite of training solutions providing a personalised learning experience for every employee.

For regionally specific benefits, your Talent Acquisition contact will be able to provide you with more information.

We’re committed to building an inclusive hiring process. If you need any adjustments or accommodations, just let us know, we’ll do our best to support you.

The team: Join our Consumer/Wholesale Team to help grow Ledger's presence and success within the APAC and US markets.

As a Channel Marketing Specialist, you will move fast and think pragmatically to execute Ledger’s marketing calendar across key retail partners. You will lead projects from the initial concept to final in-store delivery, ensuring every touchpoint reflects our premium brand.

What you’ll be doing:

  • Project Management: Lead the end-to-end creation of marketing assets, such as in-store displays and digital banners, managing the process from conceptualization to final delivery

  • Campaign Execution: Deploy global online and offline campaigns that drive sales from awareness to conversion within the US and Asia regions

  • Performance & Revenue Optimization: Rigorously monitor campaign ROI and performance data with the clear objective of maximizing revenue and identifying growth opportunities

  • Brand Guardianship: Ensure Ledger’s tone of voice and premium image are consistently maintained across all wholesale partner platforms

  • Cross-Functional & Partner Coordination: Serve as a key point of contact for external partners and internal teams (Sales, Design, Content) to ensure seamless project execution

  • Innovation: Apply a futuristic mindset by utilizing AI tools to streamline workflows and stay ahead of Web3 and NFT trends

What we’re looking for:

  • Experience: Proven experience in online and offline brand marketing, with a background in client-facing or partner-facing roles

  • Analytical Rigor: Strong business acumen and a data-driven approach to performance analysis with a focus on revenue impact

  • Pragmatism: High level of common sense with excellent organizational abilities to find practical solutions to execution hurdles

  • Tech Savvy: A futuristic mindset with an appetite for the Crypto/Web3 ecosystem and comfort using AI to enhance productivity

  • Regional Expertise: Local knowledge of the Asia market and fluency in Chinese is considered a significant plus

  • Communication: Fluent English

jobs.descriptionHtml:
About Ledger

With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.

The Ledger Wallet application is where users can manage their wallet, visualize and grow their portfolio of assets, but also where they can enjoy, with the highest degree of secure connectivity, a large span of services competing for their customership, such as buying or selling crypto, staking, trading, or choosing among crypto cards, subscription programs and other stablecoin-based products.

The Business Manager, Ledger Wallet will assist the EVP Consumer Services in his mission, own the Wallet consumers lifecycle from acquisition to engagement, bring consistency and maximize the impact of the BU towards hypergrowth by bringing clarity and coordination to all direct and indirect groups involved.

What you’ll be doing:

  • Wallet Consumer Lifecycle Ownership: Own and coordinate actions across the entire consumer lifecycle to significantly increase the number of active and engaged Wallet users.

  • Overall Wallet oversight: Bring glue and consistency of action between the Wallet’s business line. Own uncovered areas of the Wallet, particularly unmonetized sections and user flows between sections.

  • BU Strategic & Operational Efficiency: Ensure perfect coordination and efficiency among all Business Unit direct and indirect contributors (people and departments) for the attainment of revenue goals. Coordinate the Business Unit's 360-degree strategy and its concrete, timely and impactful delivery. You play the most active role in Business Unit rhythms, collaboration and governance.

  • Disrupting: The digital asset revolution has been disrupting the world of value, and digital private property is creating unprecedented opportunities. You must enjoy it and want to be part of it.

  • Consumer Understanding: Although some services are delivered by B2BC providers, Ledger Wallet will be successful as long as it is successful with customers. You must enjoy understanding them and serve them on the digital asset market. You want to have an impact on them.

  • Hyper Growth Strategic & Quantitative Work: The position demands a high degree of quantitative focus and analytical excellence, coupled with total mastery of strategy articulation, presentation skills, strategy definition, and P&L concepts, all within a high-growth environment. This has to be fulfilling for you.

  • Working with People: You will have to exert significant personal influence and inspire execution. You must combine excellence with culture, curiosity, total humility, and listening skills. You seek trusted collaborations.

  • Listen and Understand: Even more importantly, your appetite to listen and understand, go to the bottom of things with facts and people will be key in this role.

  • Organize and Clarify: A very important feature of your role, you must have affinity to it.

What we’re looking for:

  • Exceptional Quantitative & Strategic Acumen: Excellence in quantitative skills is key. You must demonstrate total mastery in strategy articulation, presentation skills, strategy definition assistance, P&L concepts, and a high-growth focus.

  • Ability to work in a matrixed environment: Inspiring and leading execution across a highly matrixed organization through clear communication, competence, data, and influence to align all resources towards Business Unit goals. You won’t lead through a hierarchical structure, nor through seniority.

  • Rigorous planning and time keeping: As for most businesses, time is more an enemy than an opportunity. You should be extremely organized and capable of exercising a strong influence on keeping the BU efforts ahead of plan and market evolutions.

  • Proven success across an outstanding academic background, abstraction, and thinking skills through the most demanding and shaping organizations in the matter.

  • Fluency in English: Although the role is based in Paris, French is not a prerequisite for either social or work life in our office.

  • AI: Preexisting engagement and strong appetite for all what AI can bring is a must.

At Ledger, we are dedicated to continually investing in our employees which is why we offer more than just salaries; we provide comprehensive compensation packages that include a wide range of benefits. Here are some of the benefits you can look forward to:

  • Flexible work options - Our hybrid policy allows employees to work from home up to 3 times per week

  • Health & Wellness support - Health and Life Insurance.

  • Financial growth opportunities - Employees can become shareholders in Ledger as well as other financial benefits depending on your country of work.

  • Commuter allowance - Ledger offers a commuter allowance to contribute to your preferred means of transportation.

  • Learning & Development - A comprehensive suite of training solutions providing a personalised learning experience for every employee.

For regionally specific benefits, your Talent Acquisition contact will be able to provide you with more information.

We’re committed to building an inclusive hiring process. If you need any adjustments or accommodations, just let us know, we’ll do our best to support you.

jobs.title: Senior Front-end Engineer - Platform
jobs.descriptionHtml:
About Ledger

We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.

The team:

Ledger Wallet is our flagship application, built on a Open Source monorepo using React, Electron (Desktop), and React Native (Mobile). It supports multiple blockchains and integrates numerous D Apps.

You will join the Platform Team, a transverse unit responsible for the "skeleton" and infrastructure of Ledger Wallet. We don’t just build features, we build the foundation that allows other feature teams to ship faster and safer.

We are looking for a Senior Engineer to help own the tech stack, improve our monorepo architecture, and drive the transition toward micro-frontends. You will treat "other developers" as your primary customers, focusing on tooling, build speed, and Developer Experience (DX).

What you’ll be doing:

  • Architecture & Monorepo Management: Set and uphold the engineering standards for our large-scale monorepo, Ledger Wallet. Drive improvements in dependency management, build performance, and structural organization so the codebase scales cleanly with dozens of contributors.

  • Micro-Frontends & Scalability: Work on shaping the technical strategy for decoupling our architecture by architecting automated CI/CD workflows and micro-frontend approaches that enable independent team deployments on both Mobile and Desktop Platforms.

  • Developer Experience (DX): Design and build internal tooling, scripts, and CLIs that remove friction for other developers. If a process is slow or manual, your job is to automate it.

  • Tech Stack Governance: Maintain the health of our core stack (React, React Native, Electron, TypeScript). Handle upgrades, minimize technical debt, and enforce best practices across the organization.

  • Mentorship & Advocacy: Act as a technical referent. Promote engineering standards, conduct high-level code reviews.

What we’re looking for:

  • Experience: 6+ years in software development with significant experience in complex Frontend architectures.

  • Stack Mastery: Deep proficiency in TypeScript and React. You understand how React works under the hood (reconciliation, rendering, performance).

  • Platform Mindset: Proven experience managing Monorepos (using tools like Nx, Turborepo, Lerna, or similar) and configuring build tools (Webpack, Rspack, ESBuild, Metro, Re.Pack).

  • Mobile & Desktop: Experience with React Native and/or Electron is highly critical for this role, as you will impact both platforms.

  • Architecture: Practical experience implementing Micro-frontends or modular architectures at scale.

  • CI/CD Ops: Familiarity with configuring pipelines (GitHub Actions, CircleCI) and optimizing build/test parallelization.

  • Experience with native modules in React Native.

  • Experience in Web3 topics and technical stacks.

  • Active contributor to Open Source projects.

jobs.title: Senior Analytics Engineer
jobs.descriptionHtml:
We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.

The team

The Data team is responsible for leveraging data to drive strategic business decisions and enhance our product offerings. As a core member, you will be responsible for end-to-end data projects—from defining metrics and building robust data pipelines to deploying analytical models. Your insights will be essential for monitoring company performance, understanding user behavior across various protocols, and contributing directly to Ledger's growth and security strategies. If you are a data professional passionate about the intersection of data science and the cutting-edge Web3 ecosystem, and you want to work in an environment that champions innovation, security, and decentralization, this role is for you.

What you’ll be doing

  • Data Architecture & Modeling: Design, build, and maintain robust, scalable data pipelines (ELT) using dbt and Airflow, ensuring our Snowflake warehouse is optimized for both performance and clarity.

  • Complex Data Mapping: Bridge the gap between raw, unstructured technical data (including on-chain events) and clean, actionable business entities.

  • Stakeholder Partnership: Act as a technical partner to Product, Engineering, and Finance teams, translating high-level business questions into technical requirements and analytical frameworks.

  • Analytics Engineering Excellence: Implement version control, CI/CD for data, and automated testing to ensure the highest levels of data quality and reliability.

  • Strategic Insights: Perform deep-dive analyses to track revenue, identify user segments, and optimize product features in an industry that moves at lightning speed.

What we're looking for

  • Experience: 4+ years (Senior level) of professional experience in Analytics Engineering, Data Engineering, or a Data Science role.

  • SQL Mastery: Exceptional proficiency in SQL. You should be comfortable with complex window functions, performance tuning, and modeling in Snowflake.

  • Modern Data Stack: Deep experience with dbt (data modeling, macros, testing) and workflow orchestrators like Airflow.

  • Python Proficiency: Strong programming skills in Python for data manipulation, automation, and building data-intensive applications.

  • Engineering Mindset: You treat data code like software—version control (Git), documentation, and modularity are second nature to you.


Bonus points

  • Curiosity for Web3: You don't need to be a blockchain expert yet, but you should be excited to learn how Ethereum, Bitcoin, and DeFi protocols function.

  • Advanced Analytics: Experience with time-series analysis, anomaly detection, or predictive modeling.

  • Blockchain Exposure: Any previous experience with on-chain data (e.g., Dune Analytics, Flipside, or indexing protocols) is a major advantage.

At Ledger, we are dedicated to continually investing in our employees which is why we offer more than just salaries; we provide comprehensive compensation packages that include a wide range of benefits. Here are some of the benefits you can look forward to:

  • Flexible work options - Our hybrid policy allows employees to work from home up to 3 times per week

  • Health & Wellness support - Health and Life Insurance.

  • Financial growth opportunities - Employees can become shareholders in Ledger as well as other financial benefits depending on your country of work.

  • Commuter allowance - Ledger offers a commuter allowance to contribute to your preferred means of transportation.

  • Learning & Development - A comprehensive suite of training solutions providing a personalised learning experience for every employee.

For regionally specific benefits, your Talent Acquisition contact will be able to provide you with more information.

We’re committed to building an inclusive hiring process. If you need any adjustments or accommodations, just let us know, we’ll do our best to support you.

jobs.title: Tax Specialist (FTC - 6 months)
jobs.descriptionHtml:
About Ledger

We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.

Reporting to the Tax Manager and working closely with operational teams, your missions are structured around three main areas:

What you’ll be doing:

1. Indirect Tax & International Operations

  • VAT: Support VAT compliance in France and the numerous jurisdictions where we operate (EU and non-EU).

  • Customs Reporting: Support the production of DEB (Intrastat arrivals/dispatches), DES (European Sales Listing for services), and Intrastat declarations.

  • Complex Flows: Analyze goods and services flows to optimize invoicing schemes and mitigate customs risks.

2. Transfer Pricing & Group Strategy

  • Documentation: Update and monitor the group’s transfer pricing policy (Master File / Local Files).

  • Intercompany: Ensure the operational implementation of internal recharging agreements.

3. Innovation Tax & Direct Tax

  • R&D Tax Credit (CIR): Good understanding of the Research Tax Credit (CIR) mechanism and its calculation methodology.

  • Corporate Income Tax (CIT): Good understanding of corporate income tax principles, including taxable income determination, tax returns, and deferred taxes.

4. Tools & Projects

  • Tools: Participate in the automation of tax processes within the ERP and tax compliance tools; contribute to the implementation of electronic invoicing reforms.

  • Excel: Build and maintain tax reporting files and reconciliation models (pivot tables, formulas, data analysis).

What we’re looking for:

  • Education: Master’s degree in Tax Law or Business School with a specialization in international taxation.

  • Experience: Significant experience in Tax, ideally gained in a Tech/E-commerce environment, a law firm, or an audit firm.

  • Technical Expertise: Good knowledge of VAT (French and international) and transfer pricing. Strong Excel skills are essential (pivot tables, formulas, data reconciliation). Experience with an ERP (NetSuite) is a plus.

  • Languages: Fluent English is mandatory (daily interaction with foreign subsidiaries, agencies, and advisors).

  • Soft Skills: Analytical rigor, agility in a fast-paced environment, and the ability to simplify complex topics for operational stakeholders.

At Ledger, we are dedicated to continually investing in our employees which is why we offer more than just salaries; we provide comprehensive compensation packages that include a wide range of benefits. Here are some of the benefits you can look forward to:

  • Flexible work options - Our hybrid policy allows employees to work from home up to 3 times per week

  • Health & Wellness support - Health and Life Insurance.

  • Commuter allowance - Ledger offers a commuter allowance to contribute to your preferred means of transportation.

  • Learning & Development - A comprehensive suite of training solutions providing a personalised learning experience for every employee.

For regionally specific benefits, your Talent Acquisition contact will be able to provide you with more information.

We’re committed to building an inclusive hiring process. If you need any adjustments or accommodations, just let us know, we’ll do our best to support you.

jobs.descriptionHtml:
About Ledger

We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.

Ledger attaches paramount importance to the protection and security of personal data. As an intern within the DPO (Data Protection Officer) team, you will be responsible for assisting the team in the company’s compliance activities.

What you’ll be doing:

  • Compliance Monitoring: Participate in the monitoring of the compliance program for each group team and in compliance actions (analysis of business constraints and risks, identification of measures to be taken, project monitoring, drafting of procedures, etc.).

  • Data Subject Requests: Handle requests from customers and employees regarding their personal data (access, deletion, rectification, etc.) and contribute to the improvement and automation of the management procedure for these requests.

  • Data Mapping: Contribute to the mapping of processing operations and data flows between internal systems and the group’s technical service providers, and update the record of processing activities (ROPA).

  • Contract Negotiation: Participate in negotiating personal data clauses in contracts concluded by Ledger with its service providers, and in the periodic compliance review of these providers.

  • Litigation Support: Contribute to the monitoring of any ongoing litigation, the definition of strategy, and the management of relations with external counsel.

  • Legal Watch: Conduct legal monitoring on current topics and draft internal communications (e.g., newsletters) for business teams.

  • General Support: Respond more generally to all questions from teams regarding personal data.

Depending on needs, you will also support all members of the legal department on a wide variety of subjects (trademark law, contract law, corporate law, etc.).

What we’re looking for:

  • Education: You have a specialization in personal data law or new technologies law (Master 2 ).

  • Experience: You have prior experience in a legal department, a law firm handling data protection issues, or a data protection authority.

  • Soft Skills: You are extremely rigorous, have a strong sense of organization, and are capable of managing multiple projects simultaneously.

  • Collaboration: You enjoy working in a team, possess excellent interpersonal skills, and are not afraid to interact directly with operational staff.

  • Languages: You are fluent in English, enabling you to communicate daily with international teams and work on contracts in English.

  • Tools: You are proficient in Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Slides).

  • Bonus: Knowledge of blockchain and crypto is appreciated but not essential.

At Ledger, we are dedicated to continually investing in our employees which is why we offer more than just salaries; we provide comprehensive compensation packages that include a wide range of benefits. Your Talent Acquisition contact will be able to provide you with more information.

We’re committed to building an inclusive hiring process. If you need any adjustments or accommodations, just let us know, we’ll do our best to support you.

With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.

The team

You’ll join the E-commerce Engineering team, responsible for everything customers experience on ledger.com.

This team owns the full customer journey - from discovery to checkout - working across a modern stack (Next.js, Shopify, Contentful, Vercel) to deliver high-performing, conversion-focused experiences at scale.

We work in a fast-paced, product-driven environment, shipping frequently and optimising continuously for performance, SEO, and conversion.

What you’ll be doing

As a Senior Frontend Engineer, you’ll play a key role in shaping and delivering the web experience used by millions of customers worldwide.

This is a hands-on role with strong ownership: you’ll lead complex projects end-to-end, contribute to technical direction, and elevate the team through high standards and mentorship.

You won’t just build features - you’ll help define what’s worth building and how to deliver it effectively :

  • Own delivery end-to-end

    Lead development of high-impact features from ideation to production

    Make pragmatic trade-offs to balance speed, quality, and scalability

    Ship frequently and iterate based on real user data

  • Drive technical excellence

    Write clean, maintainable, and performant code (TypeScript, React, Next.js)

    Improve architecture, scalability, and reliability of the platform

    Maintain a high bar for testing, monitoring, and code quality

  • Shape solutions, not just code

    Collaborate with Product, Design, and Marketing to define the right problems

    Contribute to technical decisions with a strong product and business mindset

    Focus on outcomes like conversion, performance, and user experience

  • Elevate the team

    Mentor engineers through code reviews, pairing, and feedback

    Share knowledge and promote best practices across the team

    Help drive a culture of ownership, pragmatism, and continuous improvement

  • Collaborate across functions

    Work closely with stakeholders to plan, estimate, and deliver work

    Communicate clearly on progress, risks, and trade-offs

    Contribute to a strong, transparent engineering culture

What we're looking for

  • Strong experience building production applications with JavaScript/TypeScript and React

  • Experience with Next.js or modern SSR frameworks

  • Proven ability to own and deliver projects end-to-end

  • Comfortable leveraging AI-assisted development tools (e.g. code generation, debugging, documentation, and exploration) to improve productivity, while applying strong engineering judgment to validate outputs and maintain high quality standards

  • Strong product mindset - focused on user impact and business outcomes

  • Experience in A/B testing and data-driven optimisation

  • Solid understanding of performance, SEO, and web best practices

  • Pragmatic approach to engineering—knowing when to optimise vs ship

  • Mentoring mindset with strong communication skills

At Ledger, we are dedicated to continually investing in our employees which is why we offer more than just salaries; we provide comprehensive compensation packages that include a wide range of benefits. Here are some of the benefits you can look forward to:

  • Flexible work options - Our hybrid policy allows employees to work from home up to 3 times per week

  • Health & Wellness support - Health and Life Insurance.

  • Financial growth opportunities - Employees can become shareholders in Ledger as well as other financial benefits depending on your country of work.

  • Commuter allowance - Ledger offers a commuter allowance to contribute to your preferred means of transportation.

  • Learning & Development - A comprehensive suite of training solutions providing a personalised learning experience for every employee.

For regionally specific benefits, your Talent Acquisition contact will be able to provide you with more information.

We’re committed to building an inclusive hiring process. If you need any adjustments or accommodations, just let us know, we’ll do our best to support you.

Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.

The team

The Analytics Engineering team supports Ledger’s three Business Units: Consumer Sales (hardware sales), Trust Service (insurance), and Enterprise (B2B). We transform raw data into actionable insights using a modern stack: Snowflake, SQL, and dbt for modeling, and Tableau / GA4 for reporting. The team also performs strategic analysis in Python and leads tactical AI initiatives to drive business growth.

What you’ll be doing

  • AI Enablement: Drive AI adoption by creating educational content on the Sana platform (tutorials, guides for AI Studio) and assisting non-technical teams in leveraging tools like Cursor or Google AI Studio.

  • Analytics Engineering: Handle day-to-day data requests and strengthen data foundations by reducing technical debt in our core models.

  • Data Science Automation: Industrialize and scale our "causal impact" and "pre/post" analysis frameworks using Python to transform manual tasks into standardized processes.

What we're looking for

  • Education: Master’s degree student (M2) in Data Science, Data Engineering, or a related field.

  • Technical Skills: Strong proficiency in SQL and Python for statistical analysis and Machine Learning.

  • Tools: Hands-on experience with dbt and Tableau is a significant advantage.

  • Soft Skills: Deep curiosity for Generative AI, high autonomy, and a pedagogical mindset to share knowledge across the company.

At Ledger, we are dedicated to continually investing in our employees which is why we offer more than just salaries; we provide comprehensive compensation packages that include a wide range of benefits. Here are some of the benefits you can look forward to:

  • Flexible work options - Our hybrid policy allows employees to work from home up to 3 times per week

  • Health & Wellness support - Health and Life Insurance.

  • Financial growth opportunities - Employees can become shareholders in Ledger as well as other financial benefits depending on your country of work.

For regionally specific benefits, your Talent Acquisition contact will be able to provide you with more information.

jobs.descriptionHtml:
We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.

As a Senior Key Account Manager at Ledger, you are the business owner of your region (France, Benelux, Switzerland, Nordics). You’ll manage a network of four distributors and key retail partners including Fnac Darty, Boulanger, Coolblue, and MediaMarkt, across both online and in-store channels. You are responsible for driving sustainable growth through long-term expansion and operational excellence. Operating with a high level of autonomy, you navigate complexity across multiple markets and a global organization to consistently deliver strong commercial results.

What you'll be doing

Deliver results and Strategic Ownership

  • Drive P&L-focused growth by optimizing and prioritizing investment (margins, MDF, promotions) to maximize ROI and top-line revenue.

  • Transition beyond tactical sales to build 3-year Joint Business Plans (JBP) and expansion strategies that align Ledger’s goals with partner capabilities.

Operational Excellence & Data Rigor

  • Maintain 100% data hygiene in our tools

  • Deliver synthetic, impactful insights that explain the why behind the Sell-out and Share of Business (SOB) data to anticipate market shifts, ensuring accountability for stock health and availability.

  • Lead complex, multi-layered negotiations for terms and product launches with top-tier global retailers and distributors.

Influence & Leadership

  • Independently coordinate with rest of Ledger’s teams (Brand Marketing, Product, and Supply Chain teams …) regional plan to grow hardware sales.

  • Map and influence multi-level stakeholders (C-Level, Category Managers, Supply Chain) within your partner organizations to ensure Ledger remains a "Must-Have" brand.

  • Solve complex issues independently using structured methods bringing recommendations to the Director rather than problems.

What we're looking for

  • A proven track record in Consumer hardware, managing high-growth, complex retail environments.

  • Strong understanding of Revenue Growth Management and P&L drivers.

  • A crypto or consumer tech enthusiast. You must be eager to master the Ledger ecosystem (Secure Element, Self-Custody) to speak as an expert to partners.

  • "Power User" of LLMs and AI tools to automate reporting and simulate negotiation scenarios, increasing your operational velocity.

  • Fluent in English and French. A third language is a plus.

  • A results-driven Team Player with a high degree of precision and the courage to challenge the status quo using data-backed arguments.

At Ledger, we are dedicated to continually investing in our employees which is why we offer more than just salaries; we provide comprehensive compensation packages that include a wide range of benefits. Here are some of the benefits you can look forward to:

  • Flexible work options - Our hybrid policy allows employees to work from home up to 3 times per week

  • Health & Wellness support - Health and Life Insurance.

  • Financial growth opportunities - Employees can become shareholders in Ledger as well as other financial benefits depending on your country of work.

  • Commuter allowance - Ledger offers a commuter allowance to contribute to your preferred means of transportation.

  • Learning & Development - A comprehensive suite of training solutions providing a personalised learning experience for every employee.

For regionally specific benefits, your Talent Acquisition contact will be able to provide you with more information.

We’re committed to building an inclusive hiring process. If you need any adjustments or accommodations, just let us know, we’ll do our best to support you.

jobs.descriptionHtml:
About Ledger

We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.

This role is a high-impact leadership position within the Global Communications and Marketing team, responsible for Ledger’s brand, communication, and strategic narrative for our institutional sector. This role reports directly to the EVP of Communications and Marketing, with a strong dotted line to the EVP of Enterprise Revenue, ensuring total alignment between brand narrative and commercial growth.

We are looking for a powerhouse - someone who isn't just a marketer, but a tenacious industry voice and a social leader. You will play a pivotal role in defining our Enterprise strategy, spearheading high-value institutional adoption, and establishing Ledger as the definitive thought leader in the digital asset space.

Your Mission

  • Champion the Narrative: Partner with the EVP of Communications and Marketing and the EVP of Enterprise Revenue to solidify Ledger Enterprise as the world’s most secure institutional platform and trading tool for critical digital assets and Web3.

  • Strategic Growth & Lead Gen: Architect and execute a global marketing strategy that combines aggressive lead generation with high-value institutional adoption.

  • Industry Thought Leadership: Act as a social and intellectual "face" for the business, proactively shaping industry conversations and building a robust network of institutional influencers.

  • AI-First Execution: Rapidly adapt and integrate AI tooling into the marketing stack to accelerate content creation, data analysis, and workflow efficiency.

  • Build & Inspire: Structure and manage a high-performing, agile team that thrives on cross-functional collaboration and results.

  • Market Intelligence: Stay ahead of the curve on industry trends, regulatory shifts, and emerging technologies, applying these insights to pivot strategies in real-time.

  • Commercial Optimization: Collaborate closely with Sales and Product teams on price testing and competitive research to optimize positioning and maximize revenue.

  • Empathetic Leadership: Serve as a strong, understanding partner to your team and peers, fostering a culture of continuous learning and evolution in a fast-paced environment.

What We’re Looking For

  • A Proven Industry Voice: Robust experience in Enterprise product marketing within the financial services industry with a visible track record of social engagement and thought leadership.

  • Tenacious Drive: A "get-it-done" attitude with the grit to navigate the complexities of institutional digital asset landscapes and emerging markets.

  • AI Proficiency: You are an early adopter of AI tools and can demonstrate how you use them to work faster, smarter, and more creatively.

  • Financial & Crypto Literacy: A strong, intuitive understanding of the financial industry and institutional asset management is essential. Familiarity with cryptocurrency markets is a bonus.

  • Elite Communication: Exceptional presentation skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical concepts to C-suite institutional stakeholders with ease.

  • Data-Driven Decision Making: Strong analytical skills with the ability to turn raw data into a compelling growth story.

  • Entrepreneurial Spirit: You are excited by the challenge of scaling an Enterprise business and possess the passion for innovation required to lead in a fast-paced environment.

  • Proven Leadership: Demonstrated ability to lead cross-functional teams and mentor direct reports through periods of rapid change.

Experience working in a remote or distributed team environment is a plus.

At Ledger, we are dedicated to continually investing in our employees which is why we offer more than just salaries; we provide comprehensive compensation packages that include a wide range of benefits. Here are some of the benefits you can look forward to:

  • Flexible work options - Our hybrid policy allows employees to work from home up to 3 times per week

  • Health & Wellness support - Health and Life Insurance.

  • Financial growth opportunities - Employees can become shareholders in Ledger as well as other financial benefits depending on your country of work.

  • Commuter allowance - Ledger offers a commuter allowance to contribute to your preferred means of transportation.

  • Learning & Development - A comprehensive suite of training solutions providing a personalized learning experience for every employee.

For regionally specific benefits, your Talent Acquisition contact will be able to provide you with more information.

We’re committed to building an inclusive hiring process. If you need any adjustments or accommodations, just let us know, we’ll do our best to support you.
